pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified integer overflow/underflow risks in coverage amount calculations, while SWC-107 applies because the updatePlan function violates the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern, creating reentrancy risks despite SafeMath usage. Evidence: Coverage calculations in _addNewTotals/_removeLatestTotals show arithmetic risks (Finding 9); updatePlan function violates CEI pattern by performing external calls before state updates (Finding 5); Type casting operations with uint64 castPricePerSec present truncation vulnerabilities (Finding 8)

raw.pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified integer overflow/underflow risks in coverage amount calculations, while SWC-107 applies because the updatePlan function violates the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern, creating reentrancy risks despite SafeMath usage. Evidence: Coverage calculations in _addNewTotals/_removeLatestTotals show arithmetic risks (Finding 9); updatePlan function violates CEI pattern by performing external calls before state updates (Finding 5); Type casting operations with uint64 castPricePerSec present truncation vulnerabilities (Finding 8)

llm_explanation.reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified integer overflow/underflow risks in coverage amount calculations, while SWC-107 applies because the updatePlan function violates the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern, creating reentrancy risks despite SafeMath usage.
evidence: Coverage calculations in _addNewTotals/_removeLatestTotals show arithmetic risks (Finding 9)
updatePlan function violates CEI pattern by performing external calls before state updates (Finding 5)
Type casting operations with uint64 castPricePerSec present truncation vulnerabilities (Finding 8)
